notes: Lightweight workout-reminder + streak feature. DATA + CONTRACT for the app/engine team; pure logic lives in ../engine/reminders-engine.ts. Grounded in ../research/reminders-research-report.md (106-agent deep research, adversarially verified). Design goal: capture most of the adherence benefit with the SMALLEST feature set — never make the app heavy or naggy. Game/anime framing (streak = COMBO, XP, ranks) is light flavor over real, evidence-based reminders. Everything below is either EVIDENCE-BACKED (flagged) or product CONVENTION (flagged); nothing promises fast habits (the '21-day' figure is a myth).

reminder_model.description: A reminder is stored as an IMPLEMENTATION INTENTION — the single best-supported technique (if-then plan: train [activity] at [time] on [days]). Optional coping line for barriers. EVIDENCE: implementation intentions d=0.31 durable (Belanger-Gravel 2013); plain scheduled reminders d=0.38 (Smith 2019).

fields.activity: string — what they'll do, user-chosen (e.g. 'Combo Coach', 'a Training Arcade stage', 'shadowbox 3 rounds')
fields.time_local: HH:mm — user-chosen cue time (self-selected beats prescribed; morning is optional, never forced — chronotype varies)
fields.if_then_text: auto-composed: 'If it's {time} on {day}, then I train {activity}.'
fields.coping_plan: optional string — 'If {barrier}, then {backup}.' (barrier planning improves if-then plans — Belanger-Gravel 2013)
fields.reminder_style: gentle | standard — tone of the nudge (both positive; no guilt option exists)

nudge_policy.description: ONE well-timed daily nudge by default. CONVENTION (frequency ceiling not proven by evidence): cap at 1 scheduled nudge/day + at most 1 optional streak-safety nudge; quiet hours respected; snooze available. Over-notifying is a known product risk, so v1 stays minimal.

streak_safety_nudge.description: At most ONE optional 'keep your combo' nudge later in the day IF a scheduled training day has no logged workout yet and the user opted in. Positive framing only — never guilt.

streak_model.description: The streak is framed as a COMBO. EVIDENCE-CRITICAL: a single missed day does NOT derail habit formation (Lally 2010), so the combo is FORGIVING — planned rest days never break it, and a small number of freeze tokens absorb an isolated miss. Punitive one-miss resets are AVOIDED (all-or-nothing dropout risk). Grace tolerates ISOLATED misses, not chronic ones (consecutive misses still break the combo).
streak_model.unit: training day (a day with at least one completed/verified workout, OR a planned rest day, OR a freeze-covered day)

planned_rest_days_per_week.note: Rest days are chosen by the user and DO NOT break the combo — recovery is part of training (fighter framing). This is the primary grace mechanic.

freeze_tokens.description: A freeze token ('GUARD') automatically covers ONE unplanned missed training day so the combo survives. Framed as blocking a hit, not a failure.
freeze_tokens.earn_rule: earn 1 GUARD per 7-day combo milestone, up to max_held

streak_model.break_rule: The combo only breaks after TWO consecutive unplanned missed training days with no GUARD available (isolated misses are absorbed; chronic misses are not).

on_break.note: No guilt copy. Surface the longest combo and a one-tap restart. CONVENTION: guilt copy is avoided on best-practice grounds (not proven harmful, but low-risk to avoid).

xp_and_ranks.description: Gamification adds real but SMALL durable value (Mazeas 2022 g=0.23 vs active controls) — so XP/ranks ENHANCE the reminder core, they aren't the whole thing. Kept minimal.

combo_milestones.flavor: Three in a row — the combo's live.
Two weeks — this is becoming who you are.
~66 days — where habits actually take root. Not a myth, real science.

xp_and_ranks.milestone_note: The 66-day milestone is a deliberate nod to the real habit-formation science (median ~66 days — Lally 2010), replacing the '21-day' myth.

copy_packs.description: Notification copy. Light video-game / anime flavor over a real fitness reminder. TONE: encouraging, coach/sensei-like, action-oriented — NEVER guilt, shame, or fake social pressure. Light personalization only (name, chosen activity/time) — elaborate tailoring not justified (Smith 2019). Variants rotate to fight habituation (convention).
scheduled_reminder: Time to train: {activity}. Let's get the reps in.
{time} — you said this was the moment. {activity}, go.
streak_safety: Your combo's at {combo}. One session keeps it alive — no pressure, just a nudge.
Don't let the combo drop — a quick {activity} does it. GUARDs left: {guards}.
Still time today to keep the {combo}-combo going.
rest_day: Rest day — recovery is training too. Combo's safe. 🛡
Today's a planned rest. Champions recover on purpose. Combo intact.
guard_used: A GUARD blocked yesterday's miss — combo survived at {combo}. 🛡
Took a hit, blocked it. Combo's still {combo}. Back at it today?
combo_break: Combo reset — but your best was {longest}. Every fighter restarts. Tap to begin a new run.
The combo dropped. No shame in the game — start a fresh one now?

personalization.description: LIGHT only (evidence for heavy tailoring is weak — Smith 2019). Use: first name, chosen activity, chosen time, current combo, guards held. Do NOT build an adaptive tailoring engine for v1.

metrics.description: SEPARATE engagement from behavior — a reminder that opens the app is NOT the same as one that produces a workout (Martin 2018). Track both, judge success on the second.

explicitly_deferred.description: LEFT OUT of v1 to keep the app light (see roadmap/game-plan-scheduler.md for the heavy adaptive version). None of these is worth the weight for a first version.
items: Adaptive / AI 'breakpoint' notification scheduling (cuts response time ~50% but is complexity v1 doesn't need)
Social leaderboards / rival pressure as a default

backed_by_peer_reviewed_evidence: single missed day doesn't derail habits (grace mechanics)
66-day median habit formation (21-day is a myth)
product_convention_not_proven: copy variant rotation to fight habituation
GUARD token counts and rest-day defaults
myth_rejected: '21 days to form a habit' — automaticity is a median ~66 days with range 4-335; never promise fast habits
